title: Avoid instantiating StringBuilder or StringBuffer with a character
description: Using StringBuilder or StringBuffer with a single character as an argument can lead to unexpected behavior due to automatic widening of char expressions to int. This can result in an instance with the provided int value as the initial capacity instead of the intended character.
impact: Potential unexpected behavior and increased memory usage

checks.message: Avoid StringBuilder/StringBuffer constructor with char (gets widened to int capacity) and use char literals instead of Character objects
